handoff.salientSummary: The main Tauri window now renders the shared AIRI Live2D character stage instead of the minimal launch shell. The Vue app mounts #app, initializes stage model state, loads the Cubism runtime through the existing Live2D SDK Vite plugin, and renders a nonblank Live2D canvas in the Tauri window.
handoff.whatWasImplemented: Bootstrapped the stage-tauri renderer with Pinia, vue-i18n, UnoCSS, and @proj-airi/stage-ui WidgetStage. Added a Tauri-local i18n module, initialized display model presets and selected stage model before mounting WidgetStage, loaded live2dcubismcore.min.js from generated public assets, excluded DuckDB wasm packages from optimizeDeps, added WebGPU/DOM async iterable types, and treated RUNTIME_ENVIRONMENT=tauri as a local beat-sync renderer environment so Live2D beat subscriptions no longer throw.
handoff.whatWasLeftUndone: The full Electron renderer shell, routing, auth/plugin host, channel server integration, transparent overlay behavior, and multi-window workflows remain in later mission features. The Cubism SDK download can fail on a cold cache if upstream network/HTTP2 is flaky; this matches the existing generated asset pattern and passes once cache/public asset is present.

commandsRun.observation: RED check before implementation found no WidgetStage rendering in the Tauri app.
Initial reproduction showed Vue crashing in WidgetStage setup because vue-i18n was not installed with app.use().
vue-tsc --noEmit passed after the final renderer changes.

vue-tsc and vite build passed. A cold-cache SDK download attempt failed once with ERR_HTTP2_STREAM_ERROR; after seeding the generated SDK cache from the local public runtime, the cached build path passed.
stage-shared TypeScript check passed after adding the tauri environment helper.
Tauri scaffold init passed and cargo check completed with the known placeholder dead-code warnings.
Rust build passed with the five known placeholder dead-code warnings.

commandsRun.command: Playwright renderer probe via system Brave at http://127.0.0.1:1420/
commandsRun.observation: Observed #app at 1280x800, one Live2D canvas at 1280x800 CSS pixels / 2560x1600 backing pixels, and screenshot /tmp/stage-tauri-render-unocss.png showed the default character stage.
Tauri dev app launched, stayed alive until timeout, and no longer emitted the i18n or beat-sync fatal renderer errors.
User confirmed the Live2D character works in the Tauri stage window.

interactiveChecks.result: User confirmed the Live2D character appears and works.

tests.coverage: Feature is covered by typecheck/build gates, stage-shared typecheck, app-local init, cargo build, bounded cargo tauri dev, Playwright/Brave DOM+screenshot probe, and user visual confirmation.

discoveredIssues.description: WidgetStage crashed on startup until the Tauri renderer installed vue-i18n with app.use().
The shared stage rendered zero-height canvases until UnoCSS was installed and uno.css was loaded in stage-tauri.
Live2D model mount threw Unknown environment for listenBeatSyncBeatSignal() until tauri was handled as a local beat-sync environment.
Generated Cubism SDK assets live under ignored public/assets/js and .cache paths. The Vite plugin now materializes them; a cold-cache upstream download failed once with an HTTP2 stream error.
MCP Serena/jcodemunch tools required by AGENTS.md were unavailable, so native tooling fallback was used and recorded.

deviations.whatIDidInstead: Used native rg/sed/git/apply_patch/pnpm/cargo tooling plus Playwright/Brave and user visual confirmation.
deviations.why: The MCP tools named by AGENTS.md were not exposed in the current tool list.
